//: Merge into ~/.gemini/settings.json (or a project's .gemini/settings.json). Hooks are ON by default in Gemini CLI; verified against 0.50.
//2: Gemini needs BOTH channels. `enableNotifications` is ONE switch covering 'action-required prompts AND session completion' (its own words), so on its own it cannot say which -- read as 'needs you', it painted every COMPLETED pane red. `AfterAgent` fires when the agent loop completes: that is the `done` the app reads the chime against.
//3: completes     -> chime + AfterAgent done -> the done wins  -> GREEN (finished)
//4: needs a human -> chime alone, uncontradicted             -> RED   (blocked)
//6: AfterTool is proof-of-work: it re-lights busy the moment a continued turn touches a tool, so a working agent never sits on a yellow idle dot. The command prints nothing (Gemini hooks expect JSON-only stdout).
//5: AfterAgent is the MAIN loop only -- Gemini's subagents run through a different executor and fire no agent hooks -- so subagents stay invisible, which is the house rule. Do NOT wire the Notification hook: it fires for errors/warnings/info, which are not 'blocked on you', and mapping it to needs-input rings the pane red for a warning.
